success_surface: Dynamic header detection combined with multi-column sorting and proper data type preservation during write operations
effective_behaviors: Dynamically identified target column positions by header name rather than hardcoding cell coordinates
Performed multi-level sorting with explicit primary and secondary sort keys in correct order
Handled datetime objects explicitly by converting to compatible Excel format before writing
Created fresh workbook instance rather than modifying source file in-place to preserve integrity
Preserved all existing sheets while selectively applying modifications to target sheet
Used explicit cell iteration with row/column index tracking for precise data placement
Logged validation metrics including modified range and affected row count after execution
evidence: Columns indexed dynamically via list.index() method instead of assuming fixed positions
sort_values() called with list of column names and corresponding ascending flags for multi-criteria sort
datetime check with hasattr(value, 'to_pydatetime') followed by .date() conversion before cell assignment
Workbook() created new, default sheet removed, sheets recreated with same names from original
Both Sheet1 (modified) and Sheet2 (unchanged reference) written to output with matching headers
Row-by-row, cell-by-cell writing loop with pd.isna() check for None/null handling
Final print statements confirmed range modified and rows affected for verification

memory_items.title: Dynamic Column Position Discovery
memory_items.lesson: Always locate columns by searching header names in the data frame's column list before performing operations
memory_items.generalization: Programmatic header discovery prevents brittleness when column order changes; use list.index() or similar lookup methods rather than assuming fixed A1 positions

memory_items.title: Multi-Criteria Sort Implementation
memory_items.lesson: When sorting by multiple fields, specify all sort keys as a list with corresponding order flags
memory_items.generalization: Sort operations requiring compound ordering should pass column names array and boolean ascending array to ensure primary/secondary sort hierarchy is maintained

memory_items.title: Date-Time Format Compatibility Handling
memory_items.lesson: Detect datetime objects in data before writing to Excel and convert to native Excel-compatible date format
memory_items.generalization: Check for datetime attributes on values before assignment; convert pandas datetime objects to Python date instances to preserve temporal data in spreadsheets

memory_items.title: Safe Workbook Modification Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Create a new workbook object rather than editing the source file directly
memory_items.generalization: Preserve file integrity by instantiating a fresh workbook, copying required sheets, applying changes, then saving to output path distinct from input path

memory_items.title: Complete Sheet Preservation Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When only certain sheets require modification, recreate all sheets in the output workbook
memory_items.generalization: Read all original sheets but only transform target sheets; replicate unmodified sheets exactly to maintain workbook completeness

memory_items.title: Explicit Data Placement with Null Awareness
memory_items.lesson: Iterate through rows and cells using index counters; handle null values explicitly during assignment
memory_items.generalization: Use enumerate-based loops for row/column positioning and apply pd.isna() checks to write None for missing values, preventing type coercion errors

memory_items.title: Post-Execution Validation Logging
memory_items.lesson: Report modified ranges and affected counts after successful completion
memory_items.generalization: Log success confirmation with specific modified cell range notation and record count of data rows plus headers for auditability

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Patterns
- **Dynamic Column Discovery**: Always locate columns by searching header names programmatically before performing operations. Never assume fixed cell positions.
- **Multi-Criteria Sorting**: When sorting by multiple fields, specify all sort keys as a list with corresponding order flags to maintain proper primary/secondary sort hierarchy.
- **Date-Time Conversion**: Check for datetime attributes on values before assignment; convert pandas datetime objects to Python date instances to preserve temporal data in Excel.
- **Safe Workbook Modification**: Create a new workbook object rather than editing the source file directly. Copy all original sheets, transform only required ones.
- **Null-Aware Cell Assignment**: Use enumerate-based loops for precise positioning and apply null checks during assignment to prevent type coercion errors.
- **Validation Reporting**: Log success confirmation with modified cell range notation and affected row counts for auditability.
